topic_name: Single Phase Transformer Construction - Exhaustive Exam Bank (1980-2026)
summary_points: Core Materials: CRGO (Cold Rolled Grain Oriented) silicon steel is the standard for reducing hysteresis and increasing permeability[cite: 3].
Construction Types: Core-type features a single magnetic path; shell-type provides two parallel paths for flux[cite: 1, 3].
Moisture Protection: Breathers use cobalt-chloride impregnated silica gel; blue when dry, pink when saturated[cite: 1, 3].
Internal Protection: Buchholz relays are gas-actuated and installed only on oil-immersed transformers with conservators[cite: 1, 3].
Advanced Materials: Amorphous metal cores are increasingly used in modern distribution transformers to minimize no-load losses[cite: 1, 3].

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding core-type and shell-type transformers is correct?

options.a: In a core-type transformer, the core surrounds the windings.
options.b: A shell-type transformer is more suitable for high-voltage applications.
options.c: In a shell-type transformer, the magnetic circuit is divided into two or more parts.
options.d: Concentric windings are primarily used in shell-type transformers.

questions.explanation: In a shell-type transformer, the flux from the central limb divides into two paths through the outer limbs, creating a parallel magnetic circuit[cite: 3].

questions.question: In a single-phase transformer, laminations are provided in the core primarily to reduce:
questions.explanation: Eddy current loss is proportional to the square of lamination thickness ($P_e = K_e B^2 f^2 t^2$). Thinner laminations break the current path, reducing losses[cite: 3].

questions.question: The cross-section of the core in a core-type transformer is often made 'stepped' instead of square to:

options.a: Reduce the mean length of the winding turn

questions.explanation: Stepped cores approximate a circle. This reduces the circumference of the winding, leading to less copper weight and lower $I^2R$ losses[cite: 3].

questions.question: In order to minimize the inrush current in a single-phase transformer, the supply switch should be closed at the instant when:

options.b: Supply voltage is at its maximum
options.d: Supply voltage is half of maximum

questions.explanation: Flux lags voltage by 90°. Closing at peak voltage ensures flux starts at its natural zero value, preventing core saturation and high current surges[cite: 3].

questions.question: Which material is most commonly used for the core of a modern power transformer to ensure low hysteresis loss?

options.c: Cold Rolled Grain Oriented (CRGO) Silicon Steel

questions.explanation: CRGO silicon steel has high permeability and a narrow hysteresis loop, which significantly reduces the energy lost per cycle[cite: 3].

questions.question: The Steinmetz exponent for modern transformer core material is usually taken as:
questions.explanation: The empirical Steinmetz formula for hysteresis loss ($P_h = \eta B^{1.6} f$) uses 1.6 for standard silicon steel[cite: 3].

questions.question: To minimize the weight of copper in a transformer, the window height-to-width ratio should be:
questions.explanation: A taller, narrower window typically optimizes the mean length of the turn, reducing copper volume[cite: 3].

questions.question: The laminations of a transformer are typically insulated from each other by a thin layer of:
questions.explanation: Varnish or a chemical oxide coating is used to provide electrical insulation while maintaining a high stacking factor[cite: 3].

questions.question: Which winding is most suitable for the LV side of a 25 kVA distribution transformer?
questions.explanation: Cylindrical windings are simple and commonly used for the low-voltage, high-current side of small transformers[cite: 3].

questions.question: The 'Pour Point' of transformer oil refers to the temperature at which:
questions.explanation: Pour point is critical for cold-weather operation to ensure oil remains fluid enough for circulation[cite: 3].

questions.question: In ODAF cooling, the 'D' stands for:
questions.explanation: ODAF stands for Oil Directed Air Forced, where oil is pumped specifically through the winding ducts[cite: 3].

questions.question: A 3-step core (cruciform) is used in a transformer to:
questions.explanation: Stepped cores allow for a more circular coil, which has a smaller circumference for the same area, saving copper[cite: 3].

questions.question: The noise produced by a transformer due to magnetic effects is called:
questions.explanation: This is caused by magnetostriction—the physical expansion and contraction of the core in a magnetic field[cite: 3].

questions.question: The Buchholz relay is connected in the pipe connecting:
questions.explanation: It must be placed here to capture gas bubbles rising from the main tank toward the conservator[cite: 3].

questions.question: To reduce the leakage reactance of a transformer, the windings should be:
questions.explanation: Interleaving the primary and secondary windings improves magnetic coupling and reduces leakage flux[cite: 3].

questions.question: What is the color of dry silica gel?
questions.explanation: Silica gel starts blue and turns pink as it absorbs moisture[cite: 3].

questions.question: Amorphous metal cores are thin ribbons with a thickness of approximately:
questions.explanation: Amorphous alloys are extremely thin (around 25-30 microns) to achieve ultra-low core losses[cite: 3].

questions.question: Class-A insulation used in oil-filled transformers can withstand a temperature of:
questions.explanation: Class A insulation (like paper impregnated with oil) is rated for a 105°C limit[cite: 3].

questions.question: If the lamination thickness is doubled, the eddy current loss increases by a factor of:
questions.explanation: Eddy current loss is proportional to the square of the lamination thickness ($t^2$)[cite: 3].

questions.question: On-load tap changers are typically used on:
questions.explanation: Power transformers require voltage regulation without interrupting the load, necessitating on-load tap changers[cite: 3].

questions.question: The primary purpose of providing fins or tubes on the tank surface is to:
questions.explanation: Increasing the surface area improves the dissipation of heat from the oil to the ambient air[cite: 3].

questions.question: High voltage bushings are usually made of:
questions.explanation: Porcelain provides high dielectric strength and resistance to environmental degradation[cite: 3].

questions.question: The phase displacement between primary and secondary voltages of a 1-phase transformer is:
questions.explanation: Depending on the winding direction and terminal markings (polarity), the shift is either 0 or 180 degrees[cite: 3].

questions.question: In a core-type transformer, the number of magnetic paths is:
questions.explanation: Core-type construction consists of a single continuous magnetic circuit[cite: 3].

questions.question: The conservator is a small tank that contains:
questions.explanation: It is kept roughly half-full to allow for the expansion and contraction of oil in the main tank due to temperature changes[cite: 3].

questions.question: A 230/115 V, 50 Hz transformer has a high voltage winding resistance of 0.5 $\Omega$ and a leakage reactance of 1.5 $\Omega$. The equivalent resistance referred to the high voltage side is:
Core loss of a single phase transformer is 100 W at rated voltage. If voltage is doubled and frequency is also doubled, the core loss will be:
questions.explanation: If $V/f$ is constant, $B_m$ is constant. Hysteresis loss $\propto f$ and Eddy current loss $\propto f^2$. Both increase, but specifically $P_i$ will double if only frequency and voltage are scaled linearly[cite: 3].

questions.question: A 50 kVA transformer used as an autotransformer with a 3530/3300 ratio has a kVA rating of:
questions.explanation: Autotransformer rating = $(\frac{a}{a-1}) \times$ 2-winding rating. $a = 3530/3300$. Rating $\approx 767.4$ kVA[cite: 3].

questions.question: Interleaving the primary and secondary windings primarily reduces:
questions.explanation: Interleaving reduces the leakage flux paths, directly decreasing leakage reactance[cite: 3].

questions.question: At maximum efficiency, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Maximum efficiency occurs when constant (iron) losses are equal to variable (copper) losses[cite: 3].

questions.question: If the air-gap of a transformer core increases, magnetizing reactance $X_m$ will:
questions.explanation: Increased air gap increases reluctance, reducing the inductance and thus the reactance $X_m$[cite: 3].

questions.question: If $V$ increases by 10% and $f$ decreases by 10%, the flux density $B_{max}$:
questions.explanation: $B_{max} \propto V/f = 1.1/0.9 = 1.222$. A 22.2% increase[cite: 3].

questions.question: Parallel operation of 1-phase transformers requires:
questions.explanation: Equal voltage ratios are essential to avoid circulating currents at no-load[cite: 3].

questions.question: The purpose of a 'Conservator' in a transformer is to:
questions.explanation: It provides a buffer space for oil volume changes due to temperature[cite: 3].

questions.question: The chemical used in a breather is:
questions.explanation: Silica gel absorbs moisture from incoming air[cite: 3].

questions.question: HV side windings in core-type transformers are usually:
questions.explanation: Crossover windings are suited for high-voltage, low-current needs[cite: 3].

questions.question: CRGO steel in cores results in:
questions.explanation: CRGO reduces hysteresis loss and allows for higher flux densities[cite: 3].

questions.question: Buchholz relay is only used on:
questions.explanation: It is a gas-actuated relay for oil-immersed transformers[cite: 3].

questions.question: The Stacking Factor is typically:
questions.explanation: Stacking factor is less than 1 due to lamination insulation[cite: 3].

questions.question: The central limb of a shell-type transformer carries:
questions.explanation: Total flux is in the center; it splits into the two outer limbs[cite: 3].

questions.question: Transformer oil flash point should be at least:
questions.explanation: A high flash point prevents ignition during faults[cite: 3].

questions.question: Cruciform cores reduce:
questions.explanation: Stepped cores allow circular windings, which use less copper[cite: 3].

questions.question: The breather dries air entering the:
questions.explanation: It removes moisture as the transformer 'breathes' during load changes[cite: 3].

questions.question: Best core material for high-frequency instrument transformers?
questions.explanation: Ferrites have high resistivity and permeability, ideal for high frequencies[cite: 3].

questions.question: Standard 50 Hz lamination thickness is:
questions.explanation: Thicknesses of 0.35 mm to 0.5 mm balance cost and eddy loss reduction[cite: 3].

questions.question: In shell-type transformers, outer limbs carry:
questions.explanation: The flux splits equally between the two side paths[cite: 3].

questions.question: Common cooling for small distribution transformers:
questions.explanation: ONAN is standard for most pole-mounted and smaller units[cite: 3].

questions.question: Humming noise is caused by:
questions.explanation: Magnetostriction causes the core to vibrate physically at double the power frequency[cite: 3].

questions.question: An ideal transformer features:
questions.explanation: An ideal model assumes zero losses, no leakage, and zero magnetizing current[cite: 3].

questions.question: According to YCT analysis of UPPCL JE papers, the percentage of silicon added to transformer steel to reduce hysteresis loss is approximately:
questions.explanation: Adding 3-5% silicon increases electrical resistivity, reducing eddy current loss, while also reducing the hysteresis loop area[cite: 3].

questions.question: A transformer has a maximum efficiency of 95% at 400 kW. At what condition do the variable losses equal the constant losses?

options.c: At the load where maximum efficiency occurs

questions.explanation: This is a fundamental machine principle: maximum efficiency is reached specifically when variable (copper) losses equal constant (iron) losses[cite: 3].

questions.question: In a 1-phase transformer, the EMF induced per turn in the primary is:

options.a: Equal to EMF per turn in secondary
options.b: Greater than EMF per turn in secondary
options.c: Less than EMF per turn in secondary

questions.explanation: Since both windings are linked by the same mutual flux, the voltage induced per turn ($E/N$) is identical for both primary and secondary[cite: 3].

questions.question: The primary reason for 'humming' in a transformer core, as highlighted in YCT technical series, is:
questions.explanation: Magnetostriction causes the core material to expand and contract in the magnetic field, generating the characteristic 100 Hz hum[cite: 3].

questions.question: What is the transformation ratio ($K$) for a step-up transformer?
questions.explanation: For a step-up transformer, $N_2 > N_1$, therefore $K = N_2/N_1$ must be greater than 1[cite: 3].

questions.question: According to YCT Chapter-wise papers, the 'No-load' current of a transformer usually represents what percentage of the full-load current?
questions.explanation: The no-load current is small because it only accounts for the magnetizing flux and core losses[cite: 3].

questions.question: Which test is conducted to determine the magnetic leakage and equivalent impedance of a transformer?
questions.explanation: The Short Circuit (SC) test provides the equivalent impedance ($Z_{eq}$), resistance ($R_{eq}$), and leakage reactance ($X_{eq}$) referred to the side the test is performed on[cite: 3].

questions.question: In a transformer, the dielectric strength of the oil is expected to be:
questions.explanation: Standard transformer oil should have a breakdown voltage (dielectric strength) of at least 30 kV across a standard 2.5 mm gap[cite: 3].

questions.question: A transformer core is laminated to reduce eddy current loss. If the supply frequency is doubled, the eddy current loss will:
questions.explanation: Eddy current loss ($P_e$) is proportional to $f^2$. If frequency is doubled, $(2f)^2 = 4f^2$, so the loss increases fourfold[cite: 3].

questions.question: Which winding has the highest number of turns in a transformer?
questions.explanation: Induced EMF is directly proportional to the number of turns ($E \propto N$). Therefore, the high-voltage side must have more turns[cite: 3].

questions.question: In YCT analysis, what is the 'Sandwich' winding primarily used for?
questions.explanation: Sandwich or interleaved windings are characteristic of shell-type transformers to reduce leakage reactance[cite: 3].
